title: Adams Core
wikitext:
[]: Adam's Core             Quote Post by Tsukasa » Fri Dec 17, 2004 6:56 pm If you look at the center of Adam, you can see the inidcation of a core. Do you suppose that there could be anyone in there? Maybe that is where Kaworu was found and kept secret by SEELE? Any ideas or theories as to what siginficance this may have, if any?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Dec 17, 2004 7:37 pm Adam has a core just like the rest of the Angels which were "born" from him. No one was inside his core... Angels have their own souls, just like humans do. Kaworu's origins are even more vague than Rei's however I would presume they are under similar circumstances. However it is known that Rei posesses the soul of Lilith, and Kaworu possess the soul of Adam.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Dec 17, 2004 7:40 pm And like the Evas, don't forget.

[]:             Quote Post by MAGI » Sat Dec 18, 2004 1:38 am Except Evas are born without souls.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Dec 18, 2004 1:45 am MAGI wrote: Except Evas are born without souls.

[]:             Quote Post by MAGI » Sat Dec 18, 2004 1:46 am Hey, it's relevant.

[]:             Quote Post by ChronoX » Sun Dec 19, 2004 12:20 pm Yes, eva's were born without souls but they did get them at one point. Eva's are human, just like us. Except for the fact of there height and the fact that they need to be bent to our will instead of it being of their own free will (aka the armor). The souls would be in the cores i think, though not entirely sure.

[]:             Quote Post by Tsukasa » Sun Dec 19, 2004 3:42 pm The Eva's, once born from their source (Eva 01 from Lilith and the others from Adam), they do not have any souls of their own; they are simply a shell ready for a soul. Now what is interesting about this whole thing is that the Marduk Agency (which isn't real anyway, Gendo was pulling those strings) is the fact that before the pilots are chosen, a 'soul' (the going-to-be pilot's mother) is already prepared in the Eva in Shinji's and Asuka's case. So, did Gendo purposely have Yui become Eva 01 (knowing he could reunite with her) and Kyoko become Eva 02 (since her initial contact experiment screwed her up anyway)?

[]:             Quote Post by ChronoX » Sun Dec 19, 2004 5:51 pm Yui's incident was unintentional to Gendou's point of view, cause Nakato Akagi was the one who caused it. Lyoko's incident is likely to be similar in nature. as for the rest, im sure it was Gendou's doing. But he did have all the canidates in one school, i believe.

[]:             Quote Post by Tsukasa » Sun Dec 19, 2004 10:20 pm Yui's incident was unintentional to Gendou's point of view, cause Nakato Akagi was the one who caused it.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Dec 20, 2004 10:26 am Yui WANTED to be unified with EVA-01. It was viewed as a 'freak accident' because the only other person who knew what would happen was Fuyutsuki; and it would have occurred whether Naoko had meddled or not. If you are shady on Yui's motivations, you really need to read this . And maybe this , too. (such as the 400% synch when Eva 01 ate Bardiel). Zeruel. Yes, Gendo had all of the potential pilots in the same school; all of their mothers have 'died'. I personally think that NERV selected those kids and kidnapped their mothers, saying they were dead, and held them somewhere, ready to be used as a core. Wild theory, but possible no?

[]:             Quote Post by ChronoX » Mon Dec 20, 2004 12:02 pm Where in the series did you find that fact? I thought that Nakato did it out of hate for Yui, not that Yui did it of her own accord. Theat is very interesting.

[]:             Quote Post by Tsukasa » Mon Dec 20, 2004 3:21 pm First, I apologize for mixing up my Angels in my last post. :roll: And after reading that conversation with the thought if Yui deciding to inhabit Eva 01, it is almost concrete. I never listened or read that conversation thinking that way and it makes TOTAL sense.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Dec 20, 2004 3:52 pm ChronoX wrote: Where in the series did you find that fact? I thought that Nakato did it out of hate for Yui, not that Yui did it of her own accord. Theat is very interesting.

[]:             Quote Post by MAGI » Mon Dec 20, 2004 4:44 pm Yes, I believe the same thing. Not much right to, since I haven't seen the episodes with the exposition of which. Red Cross Book wrote: It seems that this accident was actually intended by her will.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Dec 20, 2004 6:48 pm Generally speaking, whenever NGE literature says "seems" or otherwise beats around the bush, it is talking about a fact. ;) In the actual show, there is nothing vague about the point that Yui wanted to be in EVA-01. Because she's weird and cool.

[]:             Quote Post by Tsukasa » Mon Dec 20, 2004 7:24 pm Generally speaking, whenever NGE literature says "seems" or otherwise beats around the bush, it is talking about a fact. In the actual show, there is nothing vague about the point that Yui wanted to be in EVA-01. Because she's weird and cool.

[]:             Quote Post by ChronoX » Mon Dec 20, 2004 8:55 pm It wouldn't necisarilly be a bad idea, just more of a last resort like if you were in Yui's position. It would be kinda fun to go mental on a few angels.

[]:             Quote Post by Tsukasa » Mon Dec 20, 2004 9:17 pm It would be kinda fun to go mental on a few angels.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Dec 20, 2004 9:31 pm Apparently, it's a "privilege" reserved for females only.
